findings.title: Window rollover can race with concurrent sample recording
findings.impact: RecordUrmaResult publishes the new windowStartTimeMs_ with compare_exchange before exchanging successCount_ and failureCount_. A concurrent request that observes the new start can record into what it believes is the new window, but the rollover thread then exchanges those counters into the expired-window snapshot and clears them. Under concurrent Set/Get traffic this can make the previous window appear to meet min_sample_count or success-rate thresholds using samples from the next window, or lose/move new-window samples, causing spurious worker switches or missed URMA failover decisions.
findings.fix: Make window settlement and sample recording atomic with respect to each other. For example, protect the start/counter rollover and RecordInCurrentWindow with a mutex, or introduce a settling state/generation so no thread can record into the new window until the old counters have been snapshotted and reset.
